title: Inflation Calculator
summary: This page is a calculator tool provided by the Bank of Canada that uses monthly Consumer Price Index (CPI) data from 1914 to the present to compute changes in the cost of a fixed basket of consumer goods and services over time. The tool allows users to input a dollar amount and two years to calculate inflation-adjusted values, percentage change, and average annual inflation rates.
key_points: The calculator uses monthly CPI data sourced from Statistics Canada (V41690973 series)
The tool covers data from 1914 to the current available month, typically about two months prior to the current month
Basket of goods includes food, shelter, furniture, clothing, transportation, and recreation
No policy commentary, data release, or analysis is provided—only a computational utility
why_it_matters: This page provides no archival research value. It is a utility tool for calculating historical inflation effects using CPI data. It contains no macro catalyst content, no policy signals, no market commentary, and no analytical findings. It should not be archived as research content.
